title: Bump to Buildah v1.40.0
body:
Bumps to Buildah v1.40.0 and adds the `--inherits-labels` option to build and farm build man pages.

Also turn off the inherit-labels option test for now as it seems to be rather unhappy.

Issue for inherit-labels test failure: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25938

<!--

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.

comments.body: @Luap99 I've added what I think is needed for the API, however, it's been a long time since I've touched that code....

@TomSweeneyRedHat https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/b8a9b184afbc13c68d34185a60ad3dd89e6160f7 https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/6b7f718b4b97779c0f0107dd19d5a7b4df13f3be are examples on what needs to be touched. Yes I know it annoying that you need to do that now after the release. I think I said it many times before that the people making these changes should vendor (and update the code) during the release cycle and not put that on the person who cuts the releases like you do.

Also looks like you need to run `make vendor` again
Thanks @Luap99 !  Yep, this was a late add to Buildah, and we missed that it would also need to get into Podman at the time.  Thanks for the examples, I'll take a look.

Looks like the inherit labels test is failing.  The option looks like it's being ignored in this test: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/d6370d08ded7c5cf79fce958554565bf4e9709aa/tests/bud.bats#L2687

I'm not sure why, but I'm digging.  @Luap99 or @flouthoc, if you have thoughts, let me know.

```
[+0785s] # # buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} exp
[+0785s] # map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "license":"MIT" "name":"world" "org.opencontainers.image.license":"MIT" "org.opencontainers.image.name":"fedora-minimal" "org.opencontainers.image.url":"https://fedoraproject.org/" "org.opencontainers.image.vendor":"Fedora Project" "org.opencontainers.image.version":"42" "vendor":"Fedora Project" "version":"42"]
[+0785s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0785s] # #|     FAIL: buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} exp
[+0785s] # #| expected: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "name":"world"]'
[+0785s] # #|   actual: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "license":"MIT" "name":"world" "org.opencontainers.image.license":"MIT" "org.opencontainers.image.name":"fedora-minimal" "org.opencontainers.image.url":"https://fedoraproject.org/" "org.opencontainers.image.vendor":"Fedora Project" "org.opencontainers.image.version":"42" "vendor":"Fedora Project" "version":"42"]'
[+0785s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

```

OK, I think I know what the problem was.  I took a stupid pill this morning.  I made a couple of small changes to two Go files that the API uses and saved them in the wrong local branch while hopping between branches this morning.  They look for the setting of the new option and then move things along if it's found.  :crossed_fingers:
The two added files helped a bit.  It's now working on the command line, but the API still seems to be broken and ignoring  the inherit-labels option.
I missed a line in pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go, fingers crossed more tightly now.
